summary: Iteration 3: Full validation of the new JWT email/password admin auth replacing the X-Admin-Token gate. Rewrote /app/backend/tests/test_nepal_trip_api.py for the new flow — 32/32 pytest tests PASS covering auth/login, /api/auth/me, admin leads list/filter/search/stats, PATCH+DELETE with auth, public POST /api/leads, end-to-end public→admin, and removal of the old /api/leads GET (now 401). Frontend Playwright run covers /admin/login (wrong + correct creds, toasts, redirect), dashboard testids (stat-*, bycat-*, top-sources-list), JWT persisted in localStorage as nepaltrip_admin_jwt, reload-stays-authenticated, /admin/leads tabs/search/drawer/status/notes, logout clears token and redirects, and /admin redirects to /admin/login when not authed. Everything passes.

issues: Pressing the Escape key does not close the lead detail drawer (data-testid=lead-detail-drawer). The drawer can only be dismissed by clicking the overlay/close button. Minor UX nit — Radix Sheet usually closes on Escape by default; verify the onOpenChange handler is wired.

action_items: (Optional, minor) Allow Escape key to close the lead detail drawer on /admin/leads — wire onOpenChange so users can dismiss with keyboard.
(Optional, minor) test_create_lead_service_public returns a string instead of None — emits PytestReturnNotNoneWarning. Not a code bug, just a test cosmetic.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: get_current_admin returns 401 when role != admin via raise HTTPException(403). The startup event uses bcrypt and idempotently updates the password if it differs — verified by login test on a cold start; password reset path works.
server.py: ADMIN_EMAIL is .lower().strip() and login lower-cases incoming email — case-insensitive login confirmed by test_login_email_case_insensitive.

api.js: response interceptor clears the JWT and redirects to /admin/login on any 401 — good. AuthProvider boots by calling /auth/me with the stored token; clears token if invalid. Behavior verified by reload-keeps-auth test.

Old GET /api/leads is fully removed from server.py — now correctly returns 405 (Method Not Allowed) since only POST is mounted at that path. Test_get_old_leads_not_available accepts 401/404/405 and passes (405).
All admin endpoints exclude MongoDB _id via projection — no ObjectId leakage; verified.
Token TTL is 7 days; JWT_SECRET is loaded from .env via os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] (fails fast if missing). Good.
Backend uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup') — works but FastAPI recommends lifespan handler. Non-blocking.

success_rate.frontend: ~98% (all required flows pass; only minor UX nit: Escape doesn't close drawer)

test_credentials: ADMIN_EMAIL=devanshpandey8933@gmail.com, ADMIN_PASSWORD=nepaltrip123 (from /app/backend/.env and /app/memory/test_credentials.md). JWT stored in localStorage key 'nepaltrip_admin_jwt'.
seed_data_creation: Several TEST_-prefixed leads (service/package/general + TEST_E2E*, SEARCHME*) created via POST /api/leads during testing; left in DB so the dashboard has populated stats (18 total, 16 new, 2 in_progress, 0 closed at end of run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_nepal_trip_api.py now use JWT (Bearer) admin auth via fixtures admin_token + auth_headers, derived from ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD env or fallback constants. Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://adventure-nepal-hub.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_nepal_trip_api.py -v. Frontend testids: admin-login-page, admin-email-input, admin-password-input, admin-login-submit, admin-logout-btn, stat-total, stat-new, stat-in-progress, stat-closed, bycat-general/service/package, top-sources-list, leads-category-all/general/service/package, leads-search, lead-open-<id>, lead-detail-drawer, detail-status-new/in_progress/closed, lead-notes-input, lead-notes-save, lead-status-<id>. When automating the drawer, do not click the logout button while the drawer is open — Radix Sheet sets pointer-events:none on the body so the click is swallowed (this is correct framework behavior, not an app bug).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. The only minor finding (Escape doesn't dismiss the lead detail drawer) is a UX nit, not a functional regression.
